task_description: Screen each abstract for a systematic review on ML methods for automated tumour detection or malignancy classification from pathology images. INCLUDE a paper only if it presents or evaluates an ML model that classifies or detects malignancy from histopathology slides, cytology images, or digital pathology whole-slide images. Papers about prognosis prediction, treatment response, gene expression from pathology, or non-pathology imaging should be EXCLUDE.

papers.title: Multiple Instance Learning for Colorectal Cancer Grading from Whole-Slide Images
papers.abstract: Whole-slide images require weakly supervised approaches since slide-level labels are more available than patch annotations. We train an attention-based MIL model on 1,200 H&E colorectal cancer whole-slide images to grade tumour differentiation into well, moderate, and poorly differentiated categories. Macro-AUC 0.91 is achieved, outperforming patch-level CNN and clustering baselines. Inter-rater agreement with pathologist consensus is reported.
papers.topic_hint: MIL, colorectal cancer, H&E, grading, whole-slide image
papers.methodology_hint: attention MIL, 1200 WSIs, macro-AUC 0.91, patch CNN baseline, pathologist agreement
papers.claimed_contribution: Attention MIL for colorectal tumour grading macro-AUC 0.91 from WSIs.

papers.title: Deep Learning for Predicting Microsatellite Instability Status from Histopathology
papers.abstract: Pathology image analysis has shown that histological features correlate with molecular subtypes. We train a CNN to predict microsatellite instability (MSI) status from H&E slides of colorectal cancer, achieving AUC 0.84. This predicts a molecular biomarker status, not tumour malignancy presence or grade classification. The task is molecular subtype prediction, not malignancy detection.
papers.topic_hint: CNN, microsatellite instability, colorectal cancer, H&E, molecular subtype
papers.methodology_hint: CNN, MSI prediction from H&E, AUC 0.84 — molecular subtype not malignancy detection
papers.claimed_contribution: CNN for MSI status prediction from H&E slides, AUC 0.84, molecular subtype not grading.

papers.title: ResNet-50 for Binary Malignancy Classification in Cervical Cytology Images
papers.abstract: Cervical cytology screening is critical for cervical cancer prevention. We fine-tune ResNet-50 on 42,000 cervical cytology cell images from the SIPaKMeD dataset to classify cells as normal or malignant. Accuracy 0.94 and AUC 0.96 are achieved, substantially outperforming SVM with handcrafted morphological features. We evaluate across three acquisition protocols.
papers.topic_hint: ResNet-50, cervical cytology, malignancy classification, SIPaKMeD
papers.methodology_hint: ResNet-50, SIPaKMeD 42k cells, accuracy 0.94, AUC 0.96, SVM baseline
papers.claimed_contribution: ResNet-50 for cervical cell malignancy classification, AUC 0.96, 3 acquisition protocols.

papers.title: Survival Analysis from Histopathology Features in Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer
papers.abstract: Histopathological morphology correlates with survival in NSCLC. We extract patch-level deep features from resection slides of 860 NSCLC patients and train a Cox proportional hazards model to predict overall survival. C-index 0.67 is achieved. The model predicts patient survival, not tumour malignancy presence or histological grade.
papers.topic_hint: survival analysis, histopathology, NSCLC, Cox model, prognosis
papers.methodology_hint: deep features + Cox, 860 NSCLC patients, C-index 0.67 — survival prediction not malignancy detection
papers.claimed_contribution: Deep feature Cox model for NSCLC survival from pathology, C-index 0.67, prognosis not classification.

papers.title: Vision Transformer for Prostate Cancer Gleason Grading from Biopsy WSIs
papers.abstract: Gleason grading of prostate biopsies is the primary prognostic tool in prostate cancer. We fine-tune ViT-L/16 on 12,000 prostate biopsy whole-slide images annotated with ISUP grade groups 1–5. Quadratic weighted kappa 0.88 is achieved against pathologist consensus. The model outperforms CNN baselines and a previous MIL approach. Deployment on a web-based reporting interface is demonstrated.
papers.topic_hint: ViT, prostate cancer, Gleason grading, biopsy WSI, ISUP
papers.methodology_hint: ViT-L/16, 12k prostate WSIs, kappa 0.88, CNN and MIL baselines, web deployment
papers.claimed_contribution: ViT for prostate Gleason grading kappa 0.88, outperforming CNN and MIL baselines.

papers.title: Transformer for Predicting Immunotherapy Response from Spatial Transcriptomics
papers.abstract: Spatial gene expression patterns in the tumour microenvironment may predict immunotherapy response. We apply a transformer to spatially resolved transcriptomic profiles from 180 tumour biopsies to predict anti-PD-1 response. AUC 0.77 is achieved. This work uses transcriptomic data from spatial sequencing, not histopathology image analysis.
papers.topic_hint: transformer, spatial transcriptomics, immunotherapy response, tumour microenvironment
papers.methodology_hint: transformer, spatial transcriptomics, AUC 0.77 — transcriptomic data not pathology images
papers.claimed_contribution: Transformer for immunotherapy response from spatial transcriptomics, AUC 0.77, not image classification.

papers.title: Attention U-Net for Tumour Region Segmentation in Pancreatic Cancer WSIs
papers.abstract: Delineating tumour regions in pancreatic cancer slides supports downstream analysis. We train an attention U-Net on 320 annotated WSIs to segment tumour, stroma, and normal gland regions. Dice 0.84 for tumour regions is achieved. The model segments tissue regions but does not classify overall slide malignancy or produce a diagnosis.
papers.topic_hint: attention U-Net, pancreatic cancer, WSI segmentation, tumour region
papers.methodology_hint: attention U-Net, 320 WSIs, Dice 0.84, segmentation — tissue delineation not malignancy classification
papers.claimed_contribution: Attention U-Net for pancreatic tumour region segmentation Dice 0.84, not malignancy diagnosis.

papers.title: Graph Neural Network for Lymphoma Subtype Classification from Bone Marrow Biopsies
papers.abstract: Lymphoma subtype classification guides treatment selection. We construct cell-graph representations of bone marrow biopsy slides and train a GNN to classify diffuse large B-cell lymphoma, follicular lymphoma, and reactive hyperplasia from 1,400 cases. Macro-F1 0.89 is achieved, outperforming image-level CNN and patch aggregation baselines. Pathologist review confirms clinically actionable outputs.
papers.topic_hint: GNN, lymphoma classification, bone marrow biopsy, cell graph, pathology
papers.methodology_hint: cell-graph GNN, 1400 biopsies, macro-F1 0.89, CNN and aggregation baselines, pathologist review
papers.claimed_contribution: Cell-graph GNN for lymphoma subtype classification macro-F1 0.89 from bone marrow biopsies.

papers.title: Machine Learning for Predicting BRCA1/2 Mutation Status from H&E Pathology Slides
papers.abstract: BRCA mutation status influences treatment choices but genetic testing is not universally available. We train a CNN to predict BRCA1/2 mutation status from H&E slides of breast cancer, achieving AUC 0.76. This predicts a germline genotype from morphology; it does not classify malignancy presence or tumour grade.
papers.topic_hint: CNN, BRCA1/2 prediction, H&E, breast cancer, mutation status
papers.methodology_hint: CNN, BRCA prediction from H&E, AUC 0.76 — genotype prediction not malignancy classification
papers.claimed_contribution: CNN for BRCA1/2 mutation prediction from H&E, AUC 0.76, genotype not malignancy.

papers.title: Self-Supervised Contrastive Learning for Pathology Slide Representation and Tumour Detection
papers.abstract: Large pathology archives lack slide-level annotations. We apply SimCLR-style contrastive pre-training to 280,000 unlabelled pathology patches and fine-tune for tumour presence detection across 8 cancer types. Mean AUC 0.93 is achieved across cancer types with 100 labelled slides per class, outperforming supervised ImageNet-pretrained ResNet and a fully supervised baseline using the full label budget.
papers.topic_hint: contrastive learning, SimCLR, pathology, tumour detection, self-supervised
papers.methodology_hint: SimCLR pre-training, 280k patches, AUC 0.93, 8 cancer types, label-efficient, ImageNet and supervised baselines
papers.claimed_contribution: SimCLR pre-training for tumour detection AUC 0.93 across 8 cancers, label-efficient.

papers.title: EfficientNet for Melanoma Malignancy Classification in Dermoscopy
papers.abstract: Dermoscopy images encode visual features predictive of melanoma malignancy. We fine-tune EfficientNet-B5 on 33,000 dermoscopy images from ISIC 2020 for binary malignant/benign classification. AUC 0.96 is achieved, with sensitivity 0.93 at specificity 0.90. Failure mode analysis stratified by lesion size and body location is provided. This is a dermoscopy classification task, not histopathology.
papers.topic_hint: EfficientNet, melanoma, dermoscopy, malignancy classification, ISIC 2020
papers.methodology_hint: EfficientNet-B5, ISIC 2020 33k, AUC 0.96, dermoscopy images — not histopathology
papers.claimed_contribution: EfficientNet dermoscopy melanoma classification AUC 0.96, failure mode analysis, not histopathology.

papers.title: Weakly Supervised Lung Adenocarcinoma Subtype Classification from Surgical Resection WSIs
papers.abstract: Lung adenocarcinoma subtypes (lepidic, acinar, papillary, micropapillary, solid) carry prognostic significance. We develop a weakly supervised model using bag-of-patches attention on 2,300 resection WSIs to classify dominant growth pattern. Macro-AUC 0.87 is achieved, comparable to pathologist inter-reader agreement. This classifies malignant tumour subtype within already-diagnosed adenocarcinoma cases.
papers.topic_hint: weakly supervised, lung adenocarcinoma, growth pattern, WSI, pathology classification
papers.methodology_hint: bag-of-patches attention, 2300 resection WSIs, macro-AUC 0.87, pathologist agreement
papers.claimed_contribution: Weakly supervised lung adenocarcinoma growth pattern classification macro-AUC 0.87 from WSIs.

papers.title: Random Forest for Predicting PD-L1 Expression Level from CT Radiomics in Lung Cancer
papers.abstract: PD-L1 expression guides immunotherapy eligibility. We extract radiomic features from pre-treatment CT scans and train a random forest to predict PD-L1 expression levels. AUC 0.71 is achieved. This predicts a molecular marker from CT, not malignancy from pathology images.
papers.topic_hint: random forest, CT radiomics, PD-L1, lung cancer, molecular prediction
papers.methodology_hint: random forest, CT radiomics, AUC 0.71, PD-L1 prediction — CT not pathology
papers.claimed_contribution: Random forest for PD-L1 prediction from CT radiomics, AUC 0.71, not pathology classification.

papers.title: Dense Prediction Network for Distinguishing Invasive from In-Situ Breast Carcinoma in Biopsies
papers.abstract: Distinguishing invasive ductal carcinoma from ductal carcinoma in situ has significant clinical implications. We develop a dense prediction network trained on 4,800 annotated breast core biopsy WSIs to classify regions as DCIS, IDC, or normal epithelium, achieving macro-F1 0.86. The model outputs a spatial map used for reporting. Pathologist agreement on the IDC/DCIS distinction is 0.88 kappa.
papers.topic_hint: dense prediction, breast cancer, IDC vs DCIS, biopsy WSI, malignancy classification
papers.methodology_hint: dense prediction network, 4800 breast WSIs, macro-F1 0.86, IDC/DCIS classification, kappa 0.88
papers.claimed_contribution: Dense prediction for IDC/DCIS classification macro-F1 0.86 from breast biopsies.

papers.title: Deep Learning Prediction of Patient Age and Sex from Pathology Slides as Quality Benchmarks
papers.abstract: Deep learning can predict patient demographic features from H&E slides, which serves as a quality benchmark for slide preparation and scanning. We train a CNN on 120,000 slides to predict patient age and sex, achieving MAE 6 years and accuracy 0.92 respectively. This is a quality control and benchmark task; it does not detect or classify tumour malignancy.
papers.topic_hint: deep learning, pathology, age prediction, sex prediction, quality benchmark
papers.methodology_hint: CNN, 120k slides, age MAE 6 years, sex accuracy 0.92 — quality benchmark not malignancy
papers.claimed_contribution: CNN for age/sex prediction from pathology slides as quality benchmark, not tumour classification.
